seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount St John and Mount Steadman. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount St John vs Mount Steadman: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount St John and Mount Steadman.
heading: Mount St John vs Mount Steadman — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount St John, Queensland and Mount Steadman, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Steadman has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Mount St John (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount St John or Mount Steadman safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount St John has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Mount Steadman scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount St John scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Steadman scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount St John or Mount Steadman?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Steadman has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
